You would wanna have her tell you the story while you were with the car or in the car or you know, you know, whatever. So where do you use these? I mean, where are the different places that you can use them for? Everywhere you can. And so most people don't use them enough and they don't use them in as many places as they could or should. So if you have a physical environment that customers come into, they belong in the physical environment. Right. Pictorial ones, this is a very old story, but so the number one car salesman, Ford salesman for years in the Southwest is the guy who was, I got cars from when I lived in Phoenix and when I was young. And typical bare bones, the kind of car dealership, a lot of sales guys, all the cubicles are the same. Right. Standing next to their cars, with their names written on them. And there were visual patterns based on referrals. So you saw mine, and you saw all three cars I bought from him in order. Then right underneath me, you saw wife, identif- Right, two cars. You saw my dad and mom in their car. You saw my brother and car. Now you got it, and he left everybody there while he went and fetched you a Coke or a coffee. So you had time to look at it. And grasp, right? So you got two messages. Oh, and if they were friends, cause that too. So then it would be written, Pete Lullo friend of Dan Kennedy on the bottom of the picture. So you got two messages, both profound. One, you got this guy treats everybody right, or they wouldn't bring everybody. Therefore, it's unseemly of me to fight with him over price. That's message number one. So not only was he the number one salesperson in volume, he was the number one salesperson in profit margin. Right. The second message you got is, here's what you do around here. Right, here's what you do. Is you refer. That's what happens here. So those were visual, no words, testimonials, in a physical environment. Very powerful. Really powerful. You use them internally, tied to specific product service offers and promotions. So if you're having an implant seminar for your dental practice this month, your newsletter should feature testimonials all about implants. Beautiful. To support the promotion that is going on this month. You obviously use them in your marketing, all of your literature, on your websites, in your DVDs, in your books, in any kind of marketing material that you would have. If you sell person to person or person to many, you use them, has stories, and has examples that reinforce your status, reinforce the value of what you do, counter an objection, demonstrate a specific point, et cetera.
